rulings.Case Title: MHYF HOLDINGS, LLC, A DELAWARE LIMITED LIABILITY ET AL VS. CHRIS V. KELLY ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Motion calendar for October 14, 2025, line 1.2. DEFENDANT CHRIS KELLY INDIVIDUALLY AND AS CO-TRUSTEE OF THE KELLY FAMILY TRUST, LARA KELLY MOTION TO STRIKE Amended COMPLAINT is continued to November 14, 2025 for opposing party to provide properly bound and tabbed courtesy copy of opposition no later than October 31, 2025 with a cover letter reflecting the new hearing date. Unbound and untabbed copy received by the Court is available for pick-up in the Court's outbox. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified, and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: DAVID GUTEKUNST VS. LINDSAY BRENNAN ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Preliminary Injunction
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for October 14, 2025. Line 4. PLAINTIFF DAVID GUTEKUNST , TRUSTEE OF DAVID C. GUTEKUNST LIVING TRUST DTD 6/25/04 Notice Of Motion And Motion For Preliminary Injunction is continued to October 31, 2025. No later than October 24, 2025 Plaintiff to file a supplemental brief (not to exceed 2 pages) providing case authority where preliminary injunction was requested/granted under similar facts, i.e. where a landlord/licensor obtained an injunction sought herein against former tenants/licensees while an action involving a determination of possessory rights was pending. If no cases are located, Plaintiff to file a supplemental brief, stating "none." =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified, and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: GAETANI REAL ESTATE VS. GLENDA VARGAS RODRIGUEZ ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion To Withdraw Or Amend Admissions Deemed Admitted For Failure To Respond
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for October 14, 2025. Line 10. DEFENDANT GLENDA RODRIGUEZ Motion To Withdraw Or Amend Admissions Deemed Admitted For Failure To Respond HEARING REQUIRED to address (1) whether, how, and by whom the moving papers were served; and (2) whether and how the defendant, who does not read or write English, can competently attest under penalty of perjury to the truth of the contents of her declaration, which is written exclusively in English. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). rulings.Case Title: 905 COLUMBUS AVENUE, LLC VS. ROYCE CARTER ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Royce Carter To Respond To Written Discovery, Produce Documents, And To Deem Matters Admitted
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for October 14, 2025. Line 6. PLAINTIFF 905 COLUMBUS AVENUE, LLC Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Royce Carter To Respond To Written Discovery, Produce Documents, And To Deem Matters Admitted is GRANTED in part, subject to opposition. Defendant shall serve complete verified written responses without objection to Plaintiff's Form Interrogatories-General (Set One); Form Interrogatories-Unlawful Detainer (Set One); Request for Production of Documents (Set One); and Special Interrogatories (Set One) within 5 days of notice of entry of order. Truth of all matters in the Request for Admissions (Set One) are deemed admitted. No authority cited for request to compel production of documents before Defendant has identified whether any responsive documents exist, so this relief is denied without prejudice.=(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified, and the opposing party does not appear.
